name: benchmark-models
tagline: Runs the same prompt through Claude, GPT, and Gemini side by side to compare speed, cost, and output quality.
verdict: Actionable, well-gated workflow with clear stop conditions and opt-in consent for cost/telemetry actions. But its own description omits real side effects (CLAUDE.md edits with git commits, vendored-skill migration, telemetry) that live in a shared boilerplate preamble, and that ~500-line preamble is duplicated inline rather than split into a reference.

highlights.summary: Opt-in telemetry (duration/outcome/repo name) posted to a gstack endpoint via the shared preamble; off by default but a baked-in destination.
description: 'Cross-model benchmark for gstack skills.' doesn't disclose the preamble's CLAUDE.md edits/commits, migration flow, or telemetry.
description states what it does ('Cross-model benchmark...') but no trigger phrasing; usage cues live only in the body/triggers field.
